title: Control Future eagerness and execution context
description: A Future runs as soon as it is created, so defer or wrap it and pass the ExecutionContext deliberately.
body: Use when working with scala.concurrent.Future. Do not assume a Future is lazy or rely on the global ExecutionContext for blocking work. Actions: remember a Future starts on creation, so to delay it wrap construction in a def or an effect type; choose the ExecutionContext explicitly and isolate blocking calls onto a dedicated blocking pool (or use blocking { }). Validation signals include a test that the side effect timing matches expectations and that blocking work does not run on the compute pool. Risk boundary: a blocking JDBC/IO call on the global compute EC starves the thread pool and stalls unrelated tasks; eager Future construction also makes side effects fire before you intend.
